metaTitle: Import Excel File to Table Online — Free | Tablesmit
metaDescription: Import any .xlsx or .xls file into Tablesmit. The table generates instantly — headers detected, data mapped, ready to edit and export. Free, no signup.
heroHeadline: Turn an Excel file into an editable web table in seconds.
heroSubtext: Upload your .xlsx file and Tablesmit builds the full table automatically from the first sheet. Headers detected, column structure preserved, data ready to edit, style, and export.

benefits.heading: Reads .xlsx and .xls formats
benefits.body: Tablesmit uses SheetJS to parse Excel files natively. Both modern .xlsx and legacy .xls formats are supported — no conversion step needed.

benefits.heading: First sheet imported automatically
benefits.body: The first worksheet in the workbook is imported. Headers in the first row are detected and set as the table header. All subsequent rows become data rows.

benefits.heading: Fully editable after import
benefits.body: Once imported, the table is a Tablesmit table — resize columns, merge cells, apply themes, change column types, add a caption, then export in any format.

benefits.heading: 5MB limit, processed locally
benefits.body: Files up to 5MB are supported. Parsing happens entirely in the browser using SheetJS. Your file is never uploaded to a server.

steps.heading: Click Import in the toolbar
steps.body: Find the Import button in the toolbar. Click the dropdown arrow and choose 'Import from Excel'.

steps.heading: Select your Excel file
steps.body: Choose an .xlsx or .xls file from your computer. Tablesmit parses the first worksheet and builds the table.

steps.heading: Edit and export
steps.body: Your Excel data is now in Tablesmit. Style it, resize columns, apply a theme — then export to PDF, LaTeX, PNG, CSV, or back to Excel.

useCases: Importing a results dataset from Excel to style it and export as a publication-ready PDF
Taking a raw data export from a database tool (saved as .xlsx) and converting it to a clean LaTeX table
Importing a pricing spreadsheet and re-exporting as a styled PNG for a proposal document
Bringing legacy Excel data into Tablesmit to merge cells and apply proper column types
